sentences.english: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
sentences.korean: 안녕하세요. 6 Minute English입니다.

sentences.english: I'm Neil.
sentences.korean: 저는 닐이에요.

sentences.english: And I'm Beth.
sentences.korean: 저는 베스입니다.

sentences.english: Every country has its own favourite foods.
sentences.korean: 모든 나라에 고유한 음식이 있어요.

sentences.english: So, when people leave home and move to a different country, getting used to the new food there can be a surprise.
sentences.korean: 집을 떠나 다른 나라로 이사하면, 새 음식에 적응하는 거죠.

quiz.question: What does '처음부터, 맨 밑바닥부터' mean in English?

sentences.english: Food can have strange-sounding names.
sentences.korean: 음식에는 이상하게 들리는 이름이 있을 수 있습니다.

sentences.english: If you know what a toad is, it's an animal, very similar to a frog, you might be surprised to hear that a famous English meal is 'toad-in-the-hole'.
sentences.korean: 두꺼비가 뭔지 아신다면, 개구리와 매우 비슷한 동물인데, 놀라실 수도 있어요.

sentences.english: Don't worry though, it's made with sausages, not toads!
sentences.korean: 음식에는 이상하게 들리는 이름이 있을 수 있어요

sentences.english: In this programme, we're exploring people's first impressions of a new culture through its food.
sentences.korean: 이 프로그램에서 새로운 문화에 대한 첫인상을 음식을 통해 탐구합니다.

sentences.english: We'll be hearing how two women, one a refugee, the other a dietitian, adapted to the food of their new home.
sentences.korean: 두 여성이 새 고향의 음식에 어떻게 적응했는지 들어볼 거예요.

quiz.question: What does '안전지대, 편안한 영역' mean in English?

sentences.english: And, as usual, we'll be learning some useful new vocabulary too.
sentences.korean: 한 명은 난민이고 다른 한 명은 영양사예요.

sentences.english: But first, I have a question for you, Neil.
sentences.korean: 먼저 닐에게 질문이 있어요.

sentences.english: A British summer wouldn't be complete without a traditional 'cream tea' – scones served with cream and jam.
sentences.korean: 영국의 여름은 전통적인 '크림 티' 없이는 완성될 수 없죠.

sentences.english: But why are cream teas controversial?
sentences.korean: 먼저 닐에게 질문이 있어요

sentences.english: Is it because: a) they're incredibly expensive? b) people disagree about whether cream or jam goes on first? or c) the recipe was originally stolen from the French?
sentences.korean: a) 엄청 비싸서? b) 크림이 먼저인지 잼이 먼저인지 의견이 다르기 때문?

sentences.english: Hmm, I think it's b) people disagree about whether cream or jam goes on first.
sentences.korean: 음, b) 크림이 먼저인지 잼이 먼저인지 의견이 다르기 때문이라고 생각해요.

sentences.english: OK, we'll find out later.
sentences.korean: 나중에 알아볼게요.

sentences.english: Now, our first story involves Natalia, a Ukrainian refugee who arrived in the Netherlands.
sentences.korean: 나중에 알아볼게요

sentences.english: After struggling to find the Ukrainian ingredients she used at home, Natalia started a cooking business using Dutch ingredients.
sentences.korean: 집에서 쓰던 우크라이나 재료를 찾느라 고생한 후에요.

sentences.english: Here she explains to BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain:
sentences.korean: 찾느라 고생한 후에

sentences.english: It was a comfort zone that I left behind.
sentences.korean: 제가 두고 온 안락한 영역이었어요.

sentences.english: So, in the beginning it was difficult to adapt.
sentences.korean: 처음에는 적응하기 어려웠습니다.

sentences.english: I needed to adapt to local foods.
sentences.korean: 처음에는 적응하기 어려웠어요

sentences.english: Here, they don't have some of the staples we had in Ukraine, ingredients are different, and so you start working with local foods, find replacements, cook with what you have.
sentences.korean: 여기에는 우크라이나에서 먹던 기본 식재료 중 일부가 없어요.

sentences.english: At first, Natalia felt out of her comfort zone, a familiar situation in which someone feels secure and in control.
sentences.korean: 처음에 나탈리아는 안락한 영역을 벗어났다고 느꼈습니다.

sentences.english: She had to adapt to her new life in the Netherlands, she had to change in order to succeed in her new situation.
sentences.korean: 안락한 영역을 벗어났다고 느꼈어요

sentences.english: Some of the staple ingredients Natalia used in Ukraine weren't available in the Netherlands.
sentences.korean: 나탈리아가 우크라이나에서 쓰던 기본 재료 중 일부가 네덜란드에서는 없었습니다.

sentences.english: A staple is a basic and important type of food that is regularly eaten by a large portion of the population.
sentences.korean: 'staple'은 기본적이고 중요한 음식으로, 정기적으로 먹는 것입니다.

sentences.english: So, Natalia adapted her recipes, replacing Ukrainian with Dutch ingredients to create a new kind of cooking which her customers loved.
sentences.korean: 나탈리아는 우크라이나 재료를 네덜란드 재료로 대체하며 레시피를 적응시켰습니다.

sentences.english: Our second story involves Rachel Winter-Roach, who left her home in England to work on the sunny island of Tobago, in the southern Caribbean.
sentences.korean: 두 번째 이야기는 영국을 떠나 카리브해 토바고 섬에서 일하러 간 레이첼 윈터로치입니다.

quiz.question: What does '주식, 기본 식품' mean in English?

sentences.english: Here, Rachel explains some of the food challenges she faced to BBC World Service programme, The Food Chain:
sentences.korean: 영국을 떠나 카리브해 토바고 섬에서 일하러 갔어요

sentences.english: When I came here, I hadn't the faintest idea what to do with a dasheen, for example, or what to do with all these amazing fruits.
sentences.korean: 여기 왔을 때, 예를 들어 다신을 어떻게 하는지 전혀 몰랐어요.

sentences.english: So, I literally had to learn from scratch.
sentences.korean: 예를 들어 다신을 어떻게 하는지

sentences.english: But that was trial and error.
sentences.korean: 전혀 몰랐어요

sentences.english: Rachel says she hadn't the faintest idea about Tobagan food, meaning she knew nothing about it.
sentences.korean: 레이첼은 토바고 음식에 대해 전혀 몰랐다고 합니다.

quiz.question: What does '안전지대, 편안한 영역' mean in English?

sentences.english: She had to learn from scratch, meaning to start from the very beginning with no previous experience.
sentences.korean: 처음부터 배워야 했어요.

sentences.english: Rachel adapted by adding Caribbean spices and herbs, or seasoning, to food she already knew.
sentences.korean: 레이첼은 이미 아는 음식에 카리브해 향신료와 허브, 즉 시즈닝을 추가하며 적응했습니다.

sentences.english: It took time for Rachel to develop her seasoning skills, mainly through trial and error - a way of solving problems by trying a number of different methods and learning from the mistakes you make along the way.
sentences.korean: 레이첼이 양념 기술을 발전시키는 데 시간이 걸렸고, 주로 시행착오를 통해서였습니다.

sentences.english: Whether you're fleeing war, starting a new job, or enjoying a cream tea, it seems there's lots to learn about a place from its food, including Britain.
sentences.korean: 전쟁을 피해 떠나든, 새 직장을 시작하든, 크림 티를 즐기든요.

sentences.english: So, I think it's time to reveal the answer to her question, Beth.
sentences.korean: 답을 공개할 시간입니다.

quiz.question: What does '안전지대, 편안한 영역' mean in English?

sentences.english: Why is the traditional British cream tea controversial?
sentences.korean: 답을 공개할 시간이에요

sentences.english: Well, I guessed it was because people disagree about whether cream or jam should go on the scone first.
sentences.korean: 크림이 먼저인지 잼이 먼저인지 의견이 다르기 때문이라고 추측했어요.

sentences.english: Which was the correct answer.
sentences.korean: 정답이었습니다.

sentences.english: And anyone who says it's jam first is just wrong!
sentences.korean: 그리고 잼이 먼저라고 말하는 사람은 그냥 틀린 거야!

sentences.english: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learned in this programme, starting with comfort zone, a situation in which you feel comfortable and in control.
sentences.korean: 정답이었어요

quiz.question: What does '처음부터, 맨 밑바닥부터' mean in English?

sentences.english: To adapt means to change so as to successfully fit in with a new situation.
sentences.korean: 'adapt'는 새로운 상황에 성공적으로 적응하도록 변하는 것입니다.

sentences.english: A staple is a basic and important type of food that is regularly eaten.
sentences.korean: 'staple'은 기본적이고 중요한 음식으로, 정기적으로 먹는 것입니다.

sentences.english: Haven't the faintest idea means to know nothing about something.
sentences.korean: 'haven't the faintest idea'는 뭔가에 대해 전혀 모른다는 뜻입니다.

sentences.english: From scratch means from the very beginning with no previous experience.
sentences.korean: 'from scratch'는 이전 경험 없이 맨 처음부터라는 뜻입니다.

sentences.english: And finally, trial and error is a way of solving problems by trying different methods and learning from mistakes.
sentences.korean: 마지막으로, '시행착오'는 다양한 방법을 시도하고 실수에서 배우며 문제를 해결하는 방법입니다.

sentences.english: Once again, our six minutes are up.
sentences.korean: '시행착오'는 다양한 방법을 시도하고

sentences.english: Bye for now!
sentences.korean: 실수에서 배우며 문제를 해결하는 방법이에요

sentences.english: Bye!
sentences.korean: 안녕!

quiz.question: What does '주식, 기본 식품' mean in English?
